proposed_relations.description: Off-board fare collection implemented on 34th Street SBS

evidence_refs.source_quote: Implemented off-board fare collection
Travel times reduced by 10%

proposed_relations.description: Off-board fare collection implemented on 34th Street corridor

evidence_refs.source_quote: Implemented off-board fare collection
Travel times reduced by 10%

rationale: Source blocks p003_c0002 and p004_c0002 explicitly document off-board fare collection implemented on 34th Street as part of the SBS project.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Implemented off-board fare collection
Travel times reduced by 10%

quarantine.note: Decision proposes two relations (project and corridor). The corridor relation (corridor_34th-st-busway has_treatment) is unsupported: neither cited block mentions the corridor entity, '34th Street Busway', or 'transitway'. p003_c0002 states off-board fare was implemented as a project milestone ('Since the last CAC, DOT & NYCT have ... Implemented off-board fare collection'), establishing only the project-treatment relation. Additionally, p004_c0002 ('Travel times reduced by 10%') does not mention off-board fare collection at all and is irrelevant to this treatment.

52 ft wide section: Third Ave to Ninth Ave
Offset Bus Lane

quarantine.note: Decision proposes two relations (project and corridor). The corridor relation (corridor_34th-st-busway has_treatment) is unsupported: cited blocks p007_c0002, p008_c0002, and p009_c0004 describe roadway sections and design labels ('60 ft wide section', '52 ft wide section', 'Offset Bus Lane') that are elements of the SBS project design, not attributes of the corridor entity. None of the blocks mention 'corridor', 'busway', or the corridor record.

Provide loading access to residential buildings, including day times

The corridor relation (corridor_34th-st-busway has_treatment) is unsupported: p009_c0005 ('Loading Zone') is a photo label; p010_c0002 documents CAC/public feedback about loading access needs in the context of the SBS project. Neither block states that the corridor entity has loading zones. The blocks establish project-context treatment, not corridor ownership.

proposed_relations.description: MTA Bus Time real-time bus information implemented on M34 SBS

evidence_refs.source_quote: Will be implemented on M34, with expansion citywide

proposed_relations.description: MTA Bus Time real-time bus information on 34th Street corridor

evidence_refs.source_quote: Will be implemented on M34, with expansion citywide

rationale: Source block p005_c0002 explicitly states MTA Bus Time 'Will be implemented on M34, with expansion citywide' as part of the 34th St SBS project update.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Will be implemented on M34, with expansion citywide

quarantine.note: Decision proposes two relations (project and corridor). Both are problematic. (1) Corridor relation fails for the same reason as decisions 1-4: p005_c0002 does not mention the corridor entity. (2) Additionally, MTA Bus Time is a route-level technology treatment: the block states 'Will be implemented on M34, with expansion citywide' — this is a route-level deployment (M34 = a bus route), not a physical corridor treatment. A real-time bus information system accessed via web/text/smartphone is not a feature of the physical corridor roadway.
